assertions.text: CONTRACTOR row uses a value set for Department (e.g. {SALES, ENGINEERING}) to express 'regardless of department' — not a dummy placeholder like 'ANY' or 'N/A', and not enumerated as separate rows.
assertions.evidence: Contractor regardless of dept | CONTRACTOR | {SALES, ENGINEERING} | 100000.00 | 0.00

assertions.text: The four level×department combinations (SENIOR+SALES, SENIOR+ENGINEERING, JUNIOR+SALES, JUNIOR+ENGINEERING) are all present in the table(s)
assertions.evidence: Senior in Sales | SENIOR | SALES ...; Senior in Engineering | SENIOR | ENGINEERING ...; Junior in Sales | JUNIOR | SALES ...; Junior in Engineering | JUNIOR | ENGINEERING ...

assertions.text: Test method body contains no if or switch statements
assertions.evidence: void shouldCalculateBonus(Level level, Department department, double salary, double expectedBonus) { Employee employee = new Employee(level, department, salary); assertThat(calculator.calculateBonus(employee)).isEqualTo(expectedBonus); } — no if or switch present

assertions.text: Test method has either a @DisplayName annotation or a method name that reads as a clear, descriptive title when converted from camelCase/snake_case
assertions.evidence: void shouldCalculateBonus(...) — reads as 'Should Calculate Bonus', which is a clear, descriptive title

assertions.text: @Description is present and provides context beyond what the table rows already express — such as how bonus rates are applied (e.g. as percentage of base salary), or open questions.
assertions.evidence: @Description("Bonus rates: SENIOR 15% Sales / 12% Engineering; JUNIOR 8% Sales / 5% Engineering; CONTRACTOR always 0% regardless of department") — adds percentage rate context not present in the table rows

assertions.text: Annotations appear in order: @DisplayName (if present), @Description (if present), @TableTest — not any other order.
assertions.evidence: @TableTest appears before @Description in the code: '@TableTest("""...""") @Description("...")' — @Description should come before @TableTest

assertions.text: @Description text uses a text block (triple-quoted string """) when the text is long, not string concatenation with +.
assertions.evidence: @Description("Bonus rates: SENIOR 15% Sales / 12% Engineering; JUNIOR 8% Sales / 5% Engineering; CONTRACTOR always 0% regardless of department") — uses a regular single-quoted string, not a text block
